close_reason: HashMap is fine for 0-3 params; switching to ArrayVec adds a dependency for negligible gain
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1100, 1164: SecretUri stores params as HashMap<String,String>. For the typical secretx URI with 0-3 query params, this is heavier than necessary — HashMap allocates on first insert, the typical use is sequential build-up, and lookup is by string key (O(1) average but with hashing cost dominating over a small Vec linear scan).

Trigger: every from_uri() call in every backend allocates a HashMap. For short-lived secret-fetch operations (CLI tools, cron jobs) this is a measurable fraction of startup time when the backend itself is fast (e.g. env, file).

Fix: Vec<(String,String)> with linear scan in param(). For ≤3 entries, linear scan beats HashMap on every metric (memory, cache, instruction count). This is also a more natural shape for adding the iteration API requested in avkc.4.

Don't bother with smallvec unless this measurably matters; std Vec with capacity 0 (Vec::new() doesn't allocate until first push) is fine.
title: SecretUri::params HashMap allocates for typical 0-3 param URIs
